title: Spend Plan by Company Phase — Not a Revenue Forecast
subtitle: Milestone-gated use of funds across the software launch (Phase 1/2) and the hardware validation track (Phase 3 sub-stages 3.0-3.4)
description: This is deliberately a SPEND plan gated by de-risking milestones, NOT a hockey-stick revenue projection, now covering BOTH tracks of the phased business. Phase 1 (free app) and Phase 2 (premium/marketplace) are comparatively cheap, software-only stages whose milestone is user traction and conversion, not physical validation. Phase 3 (OmniDrone/IoT/PondTwin) is the capital-intensive hardware track — internally staged 3.0 through 3.4 (Definition through Commercial pilot), which is the SAME TRL-gated engineering sequence this chart described before the pivot, simply relabelled to sit inside Phase 3 rather than describing the whole company. At TRL 3 hardware with no price, no cost, and no customer on either track, a revenue forecast would be fiction. What can be honestly shown is the shape of the spend and the milestone each stage unlocks. A serious investor prefers this to a fabricated up-and-to-the-right curve.

series.name: Spend envelope by phase and sub-stage (relative, not absolute)
series.note: No dollar amounts anywhere in this series. Phase 1/2 costs (small team, hosting, content/community, no hardware BOM) are more knowable in kind than Phase 3 costs but are still withheld pending real pricing/cost-to-serve inputs — see pricing-revenue.json. Phase 3 costs depend on Phase 3.0 outputs (team size, hardware costs). Relative envelopes only, on purpose.

data.unlocks: Free app live; organic/community-led smallholder acquisition; activation and retention data; the first real MAU numbers
Free-to-paid conversion measured; marketplace and consultation revenue lines live; first real MRR
data.note: Still pre-revenue in kind, not in scale — a subscription/marketplace business is inherently cheaper to stand up than a hardware validation season.

data.unlocks: Payload arithmetic; farmer WTP; BVLOS pathway; validation MOU
Profile-vs-reference agreement — the core hardware claim proven
A full-season dataset; the right to predictive claims
First hardware-layer revenue; measured unit economics; BVLOS decision

annotations.label: Cheapest overall stage — acquisition engine, no hardware spend
Cheapest hardware stage, de-risks the most — has KILL criteria
Longest and priciest hardware stage — you cannot rush a season

the_message: Fund de-risking on both tracks first; use assumed_financial_projection in financial-projections.json for illustrative spend and revenue sensitivity — always tagged assumption/modeled_projection, never as traction. Revenue scenario chart: chart-assumed-revenue-projection.json.
revenue_projection_status: ASSUMED illustrative model in financial-projections.json#/assumed_financial_projection and chart-assumed-revenue-projection.json — replace with measured data from traction.json when available.
